title: Recovery scan reads shared inputs once per call, not once per stale session (D1)
action: Per CONTEXT D1 (docs/history/cli-performance/CONTEXT.md, profiling: recovery block ~365ms of status's ~619ms; lastDurableSettlement called 33x, each re-parsing the 495KB decisions.jsonl + capture queue + listCells). RED-FIRST: write the new checks in skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_recovery.mjs (or the existing recovery test home — find it; create following the standalone convention if absent) FIRST, run red against unmodified code, record via cells verify --passed false. Checks: (a) fixture with N>=5 heartbeat-stale sessions -> the decisions store is read exactly ONCE per detectCrashCandidates invocation (count via an injected/probe reader or fs-count on a temp store), same for capture queue and cells listing; (b) returned candidates deep-equal the pre-change function's output on the same fixture (freeze the expected shape from unmodified code BEFORE refactoring); (c) zero-stale-sessions fast path unchanged. THEN refactor skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/recovery.mjs: detectCrashCandidates computes activeDecisions(root), the capture-queue read, and listCells(root) once, threading them into lastDurableSettlement via an injected-inputs signature; keep the old 2-arg lastDurableSettlement as a thin wrapper (compute-then-delegate) so other callers are untouched. Mirror to .bee/bin, render + manifest, full run_verify. Record the measured before/after status --json wall time in the verify output (advisory print, not an assertion).

truths: decisions.jsonl parsed exactly once per detectCrashCandidates call regardless of stale-session count (asserted on fixture)
candidate output byte-equal to pre-change behavior on the fixture
status --json on this repo measurably under ~300ms after the change (advisory, recorded)
RED-FIRST failing run recorded before implementation
prohibitions: No behavior change to candidate detection semantics (D1: read-once, never different answers)
No cross-call caching, no TTL (pass-local only)

trace.outcome: Recovery scan reads shared inputs once per call: status 660ms -> 378ms median; O(1) reads pinned by fixture spy
trace.verify_output: GREEN: test_recovery: 40 passed, 0 failed (was 39 passed/1 failed pre-refactor). status --json wall time (3 runs each, median, idle box): BEFORE 660ms (635,660,671) -> AFTER 378ms (377,378,394). ~43% faster, consistent with D1's measured ~365ms recovery-block cost. render_plugin_skill_trees + release_manifest --write/--check both green (417 files match).

checks.evidence: recovery.mjs:329,379-386 sharedInputs lazily once per call, threaded via optional 3rd injected arg (:185-188). Suite 40/40; non-scaling check pins O(1) reads (countsTwo==countsSix, capture==1, cellsList==1, decisions<=2).
trace n=1 fail (4b11130734bb) 13:40:44 before n=2 pass 13:42:34; judge independently reproduced 39/1 red on HEAD lib with exact scaling counts (2-session:4 vs 6-session:12).
withReadCounts patches node:fs namespace object; all readers (fsutil readJsonl, decisions:376, cells:472) observed; exact-path matching.
listCells feature filter moved to identical in-memory predicate (:205-206); output deep-equal test passes on both old and new lib; zero-stale fast path 0 reads.
sharedInputs is a per-call let (:329), rebuilt each invocation; no module-level cache, no TTL.
test_lib_mirror 25 files byte-identical; sole other caller bee.mjs:2881 stays 2-arg default-read path.
Judge-measured 376-385ms vs pre 660ms; ~300ms aspiration not yet met (advisory only) — remaining cost is the cp-2 git block; D1 semantic goal fully met.
